title: collections/v1 frontmatter (AIP-18)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter portion of an AIP-18 collection schema or item. The doctype is selected via the `schema` discriminator: 'collection.schema/v1' (a COLLECTION.md, the schema for a class of records) or 'collection.item/v1' (an instance of one record validated against a named collection).

schema.description: Collection schema doctype. Declares the shape of items (fields, statuses, ownership, deadline, lints, identity). Composes via `extends:` against another COLLECTION.md.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Items reference this name via their `collection:` field.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of purpose: what this collection captures and when an item belongs here vs another collection.

version.description: Semantic version of the SHAPE. Bump on field/status/lint changes. Independent of the collection's content.

extends.description: OPTIONAL — relative path to a parent COLLECTION.md. Recursive composition; maximum chain depth is 8.

items.description: Either a ws:// ref to an AIP-20 work workspace, AIP-10 wiki, AIP-6 company, AIP-9 operator, or AIP-3 skill — or a relative path to a consumer workspace folder.

appliesTo.description: OPTIONAL — list of consumers this collection adapts for. Hosts MUST refuse if any binding does not resolve. Not inherited; each child declares its own scope.

fields.description: Item field schema. Merge-by-name vs parent: a child entry with the same `name` replaces the parent's (subject to type-drift refusal); new names are appended.

id.description: Stable kebab-case status id. Merge key when composing.

terminal.description: Whether items in this status are considered closed. Lints like `overdue` typically skip terminal statuses.

transitionsTo.description: OPTIONAL — allowed next status ids. If omitted, all transitions are permitted.

statuses.description: Status state machine. Merge-by-id vs parent. Children may add statuses, mark inherited statuses terminal, or narrow `transitionsTo`; they MUST NOT remove an inherited status.

initialStatus.description: OPTIONAL — default status assigned to new items. MUST refer to a status declared (locally or inherited) by this collection.

cardinality.description: How many owners an item may carry. `none` = no ownership concept; `single` = one owner; `multiple` = list of owners.

role.description: Item field name that holds the owner ref.

required.description: Whether items MUST declare an owner.

ownership.description: Ownership rules. Each leaf field overrides independently across the chain.

kind.description: Deadline shape. `none` = no deadline concept; `target-date` = single date; `window` = start+end; `recurrent` = repeating.

required.description: Whether items MUST declare a deadline value.

fieldName.description: Item field name that holds the deadline value.

deadline.description: Deadline rules. Each leaf field overrides independently.

id.description: Stable kebab-case lint id. Merge key when composing.

kind.description: Lint algorithm. `custom` delegates to a host-defined check identified by `id`.

appliesTo.description: Always '*' — items belong to one collection, so the lint always applies to all items of this collection. The field is preserved for symmetry with AIP-10's lint shape.

severity.description: Lint severity. Children may soften; governance policies MAY forbid softening below `error`.

params.description: Kind-specific parameters. e.g. { days: 30 } for `stale`; { field: 'severity' } for `required-field`.

slugSource.description: How to derive an item's slug. Either a field name (e.g. 'title'), the literal 'random', the literal 'sequence', or 'hash:<comma-separated-source-fields>' (e.g. 'hash:title,createdAt').

filingPath.description: Template for where items are filed on disk. Tokens: {collection}, {slug}, {year}, {month}. e.g. 'items/{collection}/{slug}.md'.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific extensions, namespaced under <vendor>. Deep-merged across the extends chain.

allOf.description: A collection that binds to a consumer (any non-empty appliesTo) MUST extend a parent. A standalone collection has neither field, or has extends without appliesTo.

fieldDef.description: Definition of one field on a collection's item schema. Merge-by-name. Type drift between parent and child is HARD refused.

name.description: kebab-or-camel-case field name. Merge key when composing.

type.description: Field type. Drift across composition (parent string -> child number) is HARD refused (`collection_field_type_drift`).

required.description: Whether items MUST declare this field. A child may narrow false -> true; loosening true -> false is permitted (it removes a constraint without invalidating instances).

description.description: Prose describing what the field captures.

enum.description: Required when type=enum. Children may narrow to a subset; widening to a superset is permitted (it does not invalidate instances).

items.description: Required when type=array. Recursive shape — describes the inner item type.

refKind.description: Required when type=ref. The target collection's `name`. Hosts validate that ref values point at items of this collection.

pattern.description: OPTIONAL regex constraint. Only valid when type=string.

min.description: OPTIONAL minimum. For type=number: minimum value. For type=array: minimum length.

max.description: OPTIONAL maximum. For type=number: maximum value. For type=array: maximum length.

format.description: OPTIONAL named format. Common values: email, uri, semver, uuid, slug. Only valid when type=string. Hosts MAY interpret unknown formats as advisory.

enabled.description: OPTIONAL deprecation flag. A child may set false to mark an inherited field deprecated; the host preserves the field in the resolved schema (so existing items still validate) but flags new uses via lint. Setting enabled:false on a field a child does not inherit is invalid.

item.description: Item instance doctype. Universal core (schema, collection, id, title) is the only set of MUST fields. Every other field shown here is OPTIONAL at the AIP-18 level — the resolved collection schema decides which become required for this collection's items. additionalProperties is true because collection-specific fields (declared in COLLECTION.md fields[]) appear flat at the item's top level.

oneOf.description: Canonical 99% form — just the collection's name. Host floats to the current resolved schema; drift surfaces as collection_item_schema_drift (warn).

version.description: Semver range (e.g. "1.x", "^1.2", "1.2.0"). When set, schema bumps outside the range fail with collection_item_schema_pinned_drift (HARD).

oneOf.description: Object form — pin a specific schema range. Power-user escape hatch for archival snapshots, cross-team publishing, third-party imports.

collection.description: Reference to the COLLECTION.md this item validates against. Resolution order: inline (workspace root) → local file (<workspace>/collections/<name>/COLLECTION.md) → registry (ws://collections/<name>). Unresolvable → collection_unresolvable (HARD).

id.description: Unique identifier within the collection. May be kebab-case, prefixed (BUG-1234), or hashed; the collection's identity.slugSource controls how it's derived for new items.

parent.description: OPTIONAL — containment ref. May target another item or another collection.

owner.description: OPTIONAL — owner ref(s). Single string or array depending on collection.ownership.cardinality.

status.description: OPTIONAL — current status. MUST be a status id declared (locally or inherited) by the collection.

dueAt.description: OPTIONAL — deadline value. Format depends on collection.deadline.kind: ISO date for target-date, ISO datetime for window, RRULE-like for recurrent.

attachments.description: OPTIONAL — list of attachment refs. Hosts resolve refs against the workspace's file registry.

links.description: OPTIONAL — list of cross-references to other items, knowledge entries, or external URLs.

tags.description: OPTIONAL — free-form tags consumed by retrieval, search, and grouping.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific extensions, namespaced under <vendor>. Hosts MUST tolerate unknown keys; the spec's normative fields MUST NOT change meaning.
